D3 Chemistry L5 Evaluate Reference

Semiconductor Bandgap — Eg Sets the Absorption Edge

a pure semiconductor absorbs everything above its bandgap and transmits everything below; CdS Eg=2.4 eV gives yellow, CdSe Eg=1.7 eV gives red

A semiconductor's filled valence band is separated from its empty conduction band by a gap Eg. Photons with energy > Eg are absorbed (electron promoted across the gap); photons with energy < Eg pass through. The absorption edge is at λ = 1240/Eg nm. The crystal's colour is everything below the edge that makes it through.

Drag Eg. The band diagram changes; the absorption edge moves on the visible spectrum; the swatch shows transmitted (= observed) colour. Anchor markers for Si (1.1 eV, opaque), CdSe (1.7 eV, dark red), CdS (2.4 eV, yellow), ZnS (3.7 eV, colourless).
